purpose: MCP-ready EDA analysis and verification tool contract for ICCAD Problem A.

state_model.design_id: Tools operate on a server-side loaded design. design_load stores the design under design_id; other tools default to design_id='current'.

common_notes: Net and signal names must be passed exactly as they appear in the netlist or prompt, including bus bits such as n0[1].
Path and depth analysis are combinational. DFFs are treated as boundaries by the graph builder.
For current analysis-only cases, design_write emits an unchanged copy of the loaded design.

tools.name: design_load
tools.description: Load a flattened gate-level Verilog netlist into the server-side current design state.
tools.when_to_use: Use first for every testcase, before analysis or verification tools.

netlist_path.description: Path to the Verilog netlist file.

miter_dir.description: Optional directory for generated Yosys miter files.

yosys_timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for each Yosys SAT query.

tools.name: design_write
tools.description: Write the current design to a Verilog file.
tools.when_to_use: Use when the request asks to write/output the current design.

output_path.description: Path where the output netlist should be written.

tools.notes: For the current analysis-only backend this writes an unchanged copy of the loaded design.

tools.name: analysis_count_gates
tools.description: Count all cells and report totals broken down by gate type.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests asking total gate count or gate count by type.

tools.name: analysis_last_transform_stats
tools.description: Report statistics from the most recent matching transformation or optimization without rerunning it.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests asking how many gates were just added, inserted, removed, eliminated, or merged by a prior operation.

metric.description: Statistic key such as added_buffers, removed_gates, eliminated_gates, merged_gates, or added_by_type. Use added_buffers for BUF insertion counts.

gate_type.description: Gate type when metric is added_by_type.

tool.description: Optional source tool name to search, e.g. transformation_constant_propagation.

tools.name: analysis_fanin_cone_size
tools.description: Return the number of gates in the transitive fanin cone of an output/signal.
tools.when_to_use: Use when the request asks how many gates are in a fanin cone.

output.description: Target net or signal, e.g. n15 or n31[0].

tools.name: analysis_transitive_fanin_cone
tools.description: List all gates in the transitive fanin cone of an output/signal.
tools.when_to_use: Use when the request asks to compute or list the transitive fanin cone.

tools.name: analysis_direct_fanout
tools.description: List gates directly driven by a primary input, signal, or net.
tools.when_to_use: Use for direct fanout questions.

tools.name: analysis_transitive_fanout_cone
tools.description: List all gates in the transitive fanout cone of an input/signal.
tools.when_to_use: Use when the request asks to compute or list the transitive fanout cone.

tools.name: analysis_path_exists
tools.description: Check whether a combinational path exists from source to target.
tools.when_to_use: Use for simple path-existence questions.

tools.name: analysis_path_exists_avoiding
tools.description: Check whether a combinational path exists while avoiding a specified net or gate; also supports all-paths-pass-through questions by interpreting exists=false as YES.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests saying path exists that does not traverse/pass through/while avoiding a node.

avoid.description: Net/signal bit or gate to remove from the graph.

tools.notes: For 'Does every path from A to B pass through C?', call this tool with avoid=C. If the returned exists is false, then every path passes through C and the user-facing answer is YES; if exists is true, answer NO.

tools.name: analysis_enumerate_paths
tools.description: Enumerate combinational paths from source to target, subject to path_limit.
tools.when_to_use: Use when the request asks to list every path or provide complete path enumeration.
tools.notes: For official test01-test20, path_limit=1000000 is enough to avoid truncation.

tools.name: analysis_max_logic_depth
tools.description: Compute maximum combinational logic depth. Omit both source and target for full-design maximum depth; use target only for a specific fanin cone; use both source and target for source-to-target depth.
tools.when_to_use: Use for maximum logic depth, longest combinational path depth, or critical path depth questions. For full-design/current-design questions, pass no source or target; do not pass target='current', target='design', or target='netlist'.

target.description: Optional output/net/signal for fanin-cone depth. Omit for full-design maximum depth. Do not use current/design/netlist as a placeholder target.

source.description: Optional source net/signal. Use only together with target for source-to-target depth; omit with target for target fanin-cone depth.

tools.name: analysis_gate_successors
tools.description: List immediate successor gates driven by a gate output.
tools.when_to_use: Use for questions asking gates driven by a gate or immediate successors of a gate.

tools.name: verification_functional_equivalence
tools.description: Formally check whether two signals are functionally equivalent using Yosys SAT/miter.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests asking whether two signals are functionally equivalent or produce identical logic values for all inputs.

tools.name: transformation_limit_fanout
tools.description: Insert BUF gates so selected net(s) do not exceed max fanout.
tools.when_to_use: Insert BUF gates so selected net(s) do not exceed max fanout.

tools.name: transformation_insert_dedicated_buffers
tools.description: Insert one dedicated BUF per load of a signal.
tools.when_to_use: Insert one dedicated BUF per load of a signal.

tools.name: transformation_remove_dangling_logic
tools.description: Remove gates and wires that do not contribute to outputs or sequential boundaries.
tools.when_to_use: Remove gates and wires that do not contribute to outputs or sequential boundaries.

tools.name: transformation_rename_identifier
tools.description: Rename a gate, wire, net, or signal and update references.
tools.when_to_use: Rename a gate, wire, net, or signal and update references.

tools.name: transformation_reconnect_gate_input
tools.description: Reconnect one input pin of a gate to another signal.
tools.when_to_use: Reconnect one input pin of a gate to another signal.

tools.name: transformation_collapse_back_to_back_inverters
tools.description: Collapse NOT gates followed by NOT gates into direct connections.
tools.when_to_use: Collapse NOT gates followed by NOT gates into direct connections.

tools.name: transformation_constant_propagation
tools.description: Report and simplify gates with constant inputs.
tools.when_to_use: Report and simplify gates with constant inputs.

tools.name: transformation_replace_gate_library
tools.description: Replace gates in a design or cone with an equivalent target primitive library.
tools.when_to_use: Replace gates in a design or cone with an equivalent target primitive library.
tools.notes: Target libraries include nand_not, nor_not, and_not, and and_or_not. nor_not uses direct NOR/NOT templates for common primitives including XNOR-to-NOR conversion.

tools.name: optimization_reduce_depth
tools.description: Try to reduce design or cone depth, preserving functionality.
tools.when_to_use: Try to reduce design or cone depth, preserving functionality.

tools.name: optimization_merge_equivalent_or_duplicate_gates
tools.description: Merge structurally duplicate or equivalent gates when safe.
tools.when_to_use: Merge structurally duplicate or equivalent gates when safe.

tools.name: analysis_max_fanout
tools.description: Return maximum fanout for one signal, all nets, or primary inputs only.
tools.when_to_use: Use scope='primary_inputs' when asking which primary input has the highest fanout.

scope.description: Optional scope. Use 'primary_inputs' to consider only primary input bits.

answer_style.description: Optional renderer hint. Use 'signals_only' for 'which primary input' questions.

tools.name: analysis_primary_io_summary
tools.description: Summarize primary inputs and primary outputs, optionally listing names with bit widths.
tools.when_to_use: Use for questions asking the number of primary inputs/outputs, or listing all primary inputs/outputs with bit widths. Set answer_style='inputs' when listing primary inputs with bit widths, answer_style='outputs' when listing primary outputs with bit widths, and answer_style='counts' for count-only questions.

answer_style.description: Response style requested by the prompt. Use outputs for 'List all primary outputs ... with bit widths', inputs for primary inputs, counts for count-only summaries.

tools.name: analysis_gate_info
tools.description: analysis gate info
tools.when_to_use: analysis gate info

tools.name: analysis_list_gates_by_type
tools.description: analysis list gates by type
tools.when_to_use: analysis list gates by type

tools.name: analysis_cone_gate_type_count
tools.description: analysis cone gate type count
tools.when_to_use: analysis cone gate type count

tools.name: analysis_shared_fanin_cone
tools.description: analysis shared fanin cone
tools.when_to_use: analysis shared fanin cone

tools.name: analysis_zero_length_paths
tools.description: analysis zero length paths
tools.when_to_use: analysis zero length paths

tools.name: analysis_register_paths
tools.description: Count/list register-to-register combinational paths from DFF outputs to DFF D inputs. This returns a path count/list, not a depth value.
tools.when_to_use: Use only when the request asks to list or count register-to-register paths. Do not use for maximum/longest/critical combinational depth on register-to-register paths; use analysis_register_path_depth for depth questions.

tools.name: analysis_register_path_depth
tools.description: Compute the maximum combinational depth on any register-to-register path.
tools.when_to_use: Use for questions asking maximum combinational depth, maximum depth, longest depth, or critical depth on register-to-register paths. Example: 'What is the maximum combinational depth on any register-to-register path in this design?'

tools.name: analysis_pi_to_dff_depth
tools.description: analysis pi to dff depth
tools.when_to_use: analysis pi to dff depth

tools.name: analysis_outputs_depth_over
tools.description: analysis outputs depth over
tools.when_to_use: analysis outputs depth over

tools.name: analysis_deepest_output
tools.description: analysis deepest output
tools.when_to_use: analysis deepest output

tools.name: analysis_largest_fanin_cone_output
tools.description: analysis largest fanin cone output
tools.when_to_use: analysis largest fanin cone output

tools.name: analysis_dffs_by_clock
tools.description: analysis dffs by clock
tools.when_to_use: analysis dffs by clock

tools.name: analysis_floating_or_unconnected
tools.description: analysis floating or unconnected
tools.when_to_use: analysis floating or unconnected

tools.name: analysis_cut_or_articulation
tools.description: Check whether a signal is a graph cut/articulation point, including strict PI-to-PO cut queries.
tools.when_to_use: Use scope='pi_to_po' for requests asking whether a wire is a cut between any primary input and any primary output.

scope.description: Optional scope. Use 'pi_to_po' for directed primary-input to primary-output cut checks.

tools.name: analysis_articulation_points_between
tools.description: List articulation points whose removal disconnects two named combinational graph nodes.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests asking to find all articulation points between two nets, signals, or gate instances.

tools.name: analysis_boolean_expression
tools.description: analysis boolean expression
tools.when_to_use: analysis boolean expression

tools.name: analysis_signal_dependency
tools.description: analysis signal dependency
tools.when_to_use: analysis signal dependency

tools.name: analysis_signal_symmetry
tools.description: analysis signal symmetry
tools.when_to_use: analysis signal symmetry

tools.name: analysis_signal_constant
tools.description: analysis signal constant
tools.when_to_use: analysis signal constant

value.description: Optional expected constant value, e.g. 1'b0 or 1'b1.

tools.name: analysis_find_nand_equivalent
tools.description: Find existing NAND gates whose two internal input signals form NAND(a,b) structurally equivalent to a target net.
tools.when_to_use: Use for requests asking whether there exists a pair of internal signals (a,b) such that NAND(a,b) is equivalent to a target.

tools.name: analysis_dff_enable_hold_structures
tools.description: analysis dff enable hold structures
tools.when_to_use: analysis dff enable hold structures

tools.name: verification_design_equivalence
tools.description: Check current design against original or pre-transform netlist.
tools.when_to_use: Check current design against original or pre-transform netlist.

tools.name: analysis_gate_on_max_depth_path
tools.description: Determine whether a gate lies on any maximum-depth combinational path of the design.
tools.when_to_use: Use for questions asking whether a gate is on a maximum-depth or critical path.
